entityDescription: Brock is a rural township in the northern part of Durham Region, in the Greater Toronto Area of Ontario, with a population of 12,567 (2021 census). It reaches the eastern shore of Lake Simcoe at Beaverton, about 77 km north of Toronto. Its three main communities are Beaverton, Cannington and Sunderland, linked by Highway 12 and set among farmland drained by the Beaver and Nonquon rivers. Named after Sir Isaac Brock, the township is the least urban part of Durham, mixing agriculture, lakefront living and commuter growth on the edge of the GTA.

longDescription:
Brock is Durham Region's northernmost township, reaching the eastern shore of Lake Simcoe at Beaverton, where a harbour and beach open onto the lake. That lakefront edge sets it apart from the rest of Durham: this is the region's rural, agricultural corner, a mix of farmland, small towns and cottage country on the northern rim of the Greater Toronto Area. Home to 12,567 residents in 2021, Brock sits about 77 km north of Toronto in Ontario.

The township is built around three communities strung along Highway 12. Beaverton sits on Lake Simcoe with its harbour and older brick main street, Cannington lies inland on the Beaver River, and Sunderland anchors the south end. Highway 48 branches toward Beaverton and the lake, while the Nonquon River and open farmland fill the spaces between the towns. Thorah Island lies just offshore in Lake Simcoe, reached from Beaverton's harbour, while Sunderland's annual Maple Syrup Festival marks the start of spring and the former rail corridor now carries the Trans Canada Trail across the township. Housing ranges from Lake Simcoe waterfront homes and cottages, many now used year-round, to village homes in Cannington and Sunderland and century farmhouses across the concessions.

A farming and lakefront economy on the GTA's edge shapes demand for {SERVICE}. Farm families, cottagers, retirees and commuters heading toward the wider Toronto area all need dependable {SERVICE_CATEGORY} help, and {COMPANY_NAME} provides {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} for lakefront, village and rural properties. As a {BUSINESS_TYPE} that knows north Durham, the team offers {SERVICE_LIST} with {AVAILABILITY} scheduling and {ESTIMATE_TYPE} estimates.

Winters here bring humid-continental cold with lake-effect snow off Lake Simcoe and freeze-thaw swings, while spring thaw and summer downpours stress drainage on low farmland and shoreline lots. Ice can linger along the Lake Simcoe shore well into spring, and thawing farm fields shed water slowly across the flat land. {COMPANY_NAME} plans each {SERVICE} job around snow load, freeze-thaw movement and waterfront moisture so the work holds up through the seasons on both cottages and rural homes.

Brock connects to its neighbours across the region: Uxbridge lies about 23 km south in Durham, Scugog around 30 km southeast, and Georgina sits roughly 25 km west across Lake Simcoe in York Region, with East Gwillimbury about 30 km away. Whether your property is a Lake Simcoe cottage in Beaverton, a village home in Cannington or a farm near Sunderland, {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} you can rely on. Call {PHONE} for your {ESTIMATE_TYPE} estimate.

faqs.a: Yes. Beaverton and the Brock shoreline hold many waterfront homes and cottages where moisture, ice movement and seasonal use add real challenges. {COMPANY_NAME} understands lakefront conditions on Lake Simcoe and delivers {SERVICE} and {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} built to last on exposed shoreline lots.

faqs.q: How does lake-effect snow off Lake Simcoe affect {SERVICE}?
faqs.a: Brock catches lake-effect snow and sharp freeze-thaw swings off Lake Simcoe, which stress roofs, drainage and foundations on both farms and lakefront homes. {COMPANY_NAME} plans every {SERVICE} job around snow load and freeze-thaw movement so the result holds up through north-Durham winters.

faqs.q: Can you reach rural farm properties in Brock?
faqs.a: Yes. Much of Brock is farmland along the Beaver and Nonquon rivers between Beaverton, Cannington and Sunderland, and {COMPANY_NAME} regularly serves rural and agricultural sites. The team offers {AVAILABILITY} scheduling so you get timely {SERVICE} wherever your property sits. Call {PHONE}.
